questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the Sudarshana Chakra is the divine weapon of which deity?
questions.explanation: The Sudarshana Chakra is the spinning discus most closely associated with Vishnu, the preserver.

questions.q: What kind of weapon is the Sudarshana Chakra?
questions.explanation: The Sudarshana Chakra is described as a spinning, razor-edged discus or wheel.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu is most famously depicted wielding the Sudarshana Chakra?
questions.explanation: Krishna, an avatar of Vishnu, is frequently shown wielding the Sudarshana Chakra to uphold dharma.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the Sudarshana Chakra blazes with the brilliance of what celestial body?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe the Sudarshana Chakra as a wheel blazing like the sun.

questions.q: In one Puranic account, which divine architect fashioned the Sudarshana Chakra?
questions.explanation: Puranic tradition credits the divine architect Vishvakarma with fashioning the discus from Surya's surplus radiance.

questions.q: In one account, the Sudarshana Chakra was fashioned from the surplus brilliance of which deity?
questions.explanation: One Puranic tradition holds that the discus was forged from the excess radiance of Surya, the sun-god.

questions.q: The Sudarshana Chakra is most often depicted on which of Vishnu's four hands?
questions.explanation: In iconography the Sudarshana Chakra is typically shown spinning upon the index finger of one of Vishnu's hands.

questions.q: What is the primary purpose for which Vishnu wields the Sudarshana Chakra in the texts?
options: To destroy demons and uphold dharma
questions.explanation: The discus is wielded to behead demons and uphold dharma, the cosmic order.

questions.q: Alongside the Sudarshana Chakra, which other emblem is among Vishnu's classic four attributes?
questions.explanation: Vishnu's four classic attributes are the conch, discus, mace, and lotus.

questions.q: The name 'Sudarshana' is often understood to mean what?
options: Auspicious vision or good to behold
questions.explanation: 'Sudarshana' is commonly interpreted as 'auspicious vision' or 'that which is good to behold'.

questions.q: In the Puranas, how many spokes is the Sudarshana Chakra often said to have?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe the Sudarshana Chakra as a wheel of a thousand spokes.

questions.q: What distinctive property does the Sudarshana Chakra display after each strike?
options: It returns to the wielder's hand
questions.explanation: The Sudarshana Chakra is described as returning to the hand after each strike.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, Krishna uses the Sudarshana Chakra to behead which arrogant king at Yudhishthira's Rajasuya sacrifice?
questions.explanation: In the Mahabharata, Krishna beheads Shishupala with the Sudarshana Chakra after his repeated insults.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, the sage Durvasa is pursued by the Sudarshana Chakra after offending which devotee of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: When Durvasa wronged the devout King Ambarisha, the Sudarshana Chakra pursued the sage relentlessly.

questions.q: In the story of Ambarisha and Durvasa, how is the sage finally released from the pursuing Sudarshana Chakra?
options: By returning to seek Ambarisha's forgiveness
questions.explanation: Durvasa is spared only when he returns and begs the pardon of the devotee Ambarisha.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, Vishnu wields the Sudarshana Chakra to sever the head of which being who had stolen a sip of amrita, an act linked to the eclipse legend?
questions.explanation: After Rahu slipped among the gods to drink the nectar, Vishnu severed his head with the Sudarshana Chakra, and the head became the eclipse-causing Rahu.

questions.q: Which mace is named among Vishnu's attributes alongside the Sudarshana Chakra?
questions.explanation: Vishnu's mace is named Kaumodaki, held alongside the Sudarshana Chakra in his iconography.

questions.q: The Sudarshana Chakra belongs to a broader class of legendary weapons in Hindu epics known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Sudarshana Chakra is counted among the astras, the divine weapons of the gods and heroes.

questions.q: Which bird-mount of Vishnu is closely associated with him alongside the Sudarshana Chakra in iconography?
questions.explanation: Vishnu's mount is Garuda, the great eagle, often shown bearing him along with his discus.

questions.q: In which language does the word 'chakra' in 'Sudarshana Chakra' literally mean 'wheel' or 'discus'?
questions.explanation: 'Chakra' is the Sanskrit word for wheel or discus, the form of this weapon.

questions.q: In South Indian temple worship, the Sudarshana Chakra is often venerated as a deity in its own right under what name?
questions.explanation: In many South Indian temples the discus is worshipped as Chakrathazhwar, the personified Sudarshana.

questions.q: The Sudarshana Chakra is regarded in the texts as the foremost of what?
questions.explanation: The discus is described as the foremost of Vishnu's divine attributes.

questions.q: In one well-known tradition, the Sudarshana Chakra was forged when Vishvakarma trimmed the excess radiance of Surya, whose wife had complained of his unbearable glare. Who was that wife?
questions.explanation: Surya's wife Sanjna could not bear his brilliance, so Vishvakarma pared it down, and the discus was made from the surplus.

questions.q: In a Mahabharata episode, Krishna is said to have invoked the Sudarshana Chakra to veil the sun and create a false dusk, helping Arjuna fulfil a vow to slay which warrior?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Krishna used the discus to obscure the sun, drawing out Jayadratha so Arjuna could slay him.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, Krishna receives the Sudarshana Chakra from the fire-god Agni during the burning of which forest?
questions.explanation: In the Mahabharata, Krishna obtains the Sudarshana Chakra from Agni at the burning of the Khandava forest.

questions.q: Which classification of weapon best describes the Sudarshana Chakra in the traditional categories of armaments?
questions.explanation: As a discus that is thrown and returns, the Sudarshana Chakra is classed among the mukta or hurled weapons.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, who narrates to King Parikshit the deeds of Krishna, including the discus-slaying of demons?
questions.explanation: The sage Shukadeva recites the Bhagavata Purana, including Krishna's exploits, to King Parikshit.

questions.q: On Vishnu's serpent-couch in the cosmic ocean, the Sudarshana Chakra is depicted near him; on which serpent does Vishnu recline?
questions.explanation: Vishnu reclines upon the cosmic serpent Shesha, also called Ananta, with his discus among his emblems.

questions.q: Which consort of Vishnu is enshrined alongside him in the iconography where he bears the Sudarshana Chakra?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi, goddess of fortune, is Vishnu's consort and accompanies him in his iconography with the discus.

questions.q: In Vaishnava temple worship, the Sudarshana Chakra is frequently paired on the rear of a slab with which other personified emblem of Vishnu?
options: The mace as a separate god
questions.explanation: Temple icons often show Sudarshana on one face and the personified conch Panchajanya on the reverse.
